title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
description: Given a weighted directed graph, find the shortest path from source node 0 to all other nodes. Return an array of shortest distances. Use -1 for unreachable nodes.
key_concept: Min-heap BFS — always expand the unvisited node with smallest known distance. Greedy: once a node is settled, its distance is final.
when_best: Shortest path with non-negative edge weights. O((V+E) log V) vs O(V²) for dense graphs

hints.L1: Initialize dist[0]=0, all others infinity. Use min-heap of (dist, node). Pop minimum, skip if already settled.
hints.L2: For each neighbor v of u: if dist[u]+weight < dist[v]: update dist[v], push (dist[v], v) to heap. Old (larger dist, v) entries in heap are ignored when popped.
hints.L3: heap=[(0,src)]; dist=[inf]*n; dist[src]=0; while heap: d,u=heappop(heap); if d>dist[u]: continue; for v,w in graph[u]: if dist[u]+w<dist[v]: dist[v]=dist[u]+w; heappush(heap,(dist[v],v))
